question_text: A 2 kg block slides across a rough wooden surface. The friction force is measured as 6 N. The same block is then placed on a smoother plastic surface and the friction force is measured as 3 N. Assuming the normal force remains the same in both cases, calculate the coefficient of friction for each surface and explain which surface is rougher.
correct_answer: Normal force = weight = 2 kg × 10 N/kg = 20 N. Wooden surface: coefficient = 6 N ÷ 20 N = 0.3. Plastic surface: coefficient = 3 N ÷ 20 N = 0.15. The wooden surface is rougher because it has a higher coefficient of friction (0.3 > 0.15), meaning friction increases more with the same normal force.
explanation: The coefficient of friction is friction force divided by normal force. A higher coefficient means the surface is rougher and creates more friction. Rough surfaces have higher coefficients than smooth surfaces.
